description:
You are an operations engineer who has just finished triaging a production-incident report stored in 
/home/user/websvc/incidents/2024-05-17_cpu_spike.txt.  
In order to close the loop you must:

1. Perform a patch-level semantic-version bump of the service that lives in /home/user/websvc.  
   • The file /home/user/websvc/package.json currently contains a top-level key named "version" with the value "2.5.3".  
   • Bump that value to "2.5.4".  

2. Update the project changelog that lives at /home/user/websvc/CHANGELOG.md.  
   • Prepend the following exact block (including blank line after it) to the very top of the file:

```
## [2.5.4] - 2024-05-17
### Fixed
- Resolve high CPU usage incident #423
```

   • Do not alter the older entries that are already in the file.

3. Create a plain-text audit file at /home/user/websvc/bump_audit.txt that contains exactly two lines:

```
VersionBefore: 2.5.3
VersionAfter: 2.5.4
```

Formatting requirements that will be automatically verified:

A. package.json must contain the string:  
   "version": "2.5.4"

B. The very first non-comment line of /home/user/websvc/CHANGELOG.md must be  
   ## [2.5.4] - 2024-05-17

C. /home/user/websvc/bump_audit.txt must exist and its full, trimmed contents must be exactly:

VersionBefore: 2.5.3  
VersionAfter: 2.5.4

You should need no more than three or four shell commands (e.g., sed, echo or printf) to accomplish everything.  
When you are finished, verify that all three verification points (A-C) are satisfied.

No additional files or directories are required for verification.
